title: CodegenBundle document
description: Canonical field grammar of a CodegenBundle: the typed output of target-specific code generation for one optimization unit (files[], entrypoints[], target_lowering_plan, capability_requirements, state_bindings[]). The enforcement point is tools/codegen_bundle.py:validate_bundle, which holds the same facts as module constants (the unit suite pins the two copies together) and therefore cannot fail-open when this file is missing or unreadable; it additionally holds the rules no draft-07 pattern can carry (closed vocabularies, cross-field invariants). No workflow phase produces a bundle yet: the producer arrives with Z2 (docs/design/zero_base_architecture.md); this schema pins the contract beforehand. Every object is closed (additionalProperties: false) except the declared A5 extension point — the value objects of target_lowering_plan (precision, data_layout, parallelization, decomposition, communication, accelerator_mapping), whose interiors a target backend defines. That closure, together with the file-role and logical_path rules and a build graph with no command slot, is what structurally denies the bundle any build or shell authority; the extension point does not weaken it, because no consumer executes a lowering-plan value.

bundle_schema_version.description: Semantic version of this contract. The pattern pins the supported MAJOR (1), so a schema-only consumer rejects an incompatible major here rather than admitting it to fail later at validate_bundle; the canonical validator additionally reports a malformed version and an unsupported major as distinct clauses.

optimization_unit.description: The semantic nodes generated as this one bundle. The ordered member list IS the unit identity; no derived unit_id exists.

members.description: Ordered node_key list, length >= 1, duplicate-free. A single-member unit is the default.

files.description: The complete generated source-file set. Non-empty. Multiple compilation units, private helper procedures, and internal modules are permitted.

logical_path.description: Path relative to the assembled source root: normalized, POSIX-separated, confined (no leading '/', no '.'/'..' segment), unique within the bundle after case folding, with a language-allowed extension and no reserved build filename. The full rule set is tools/codegen_bundle.py:logical_path_violations; this pattern bears the per-segment grammar only.

role.description: The file's build and visibility role. There is no runner/glue role (contract-boundary glue is host-rendered and can never be bundle content) and no build/script role (the backbone of the no-arbitrary-command rule). Only helper and internal_module may be private (they cannot define an entrypoint) and only they may be shared across unit members (member_node_key: null).

language.description: Source language. v1 is fortran only; a new language is added by extending this enum and LANGUAGE_EXTENSION_ALLOWLIST together.

member_node_key.description: The unit member this file belongs to, or null for a file shared by the whole unit. null is admissible only for role helper / internal_module; a model or checks file belongs to exactly one member.

content.description: The complete source text, inline. A detached-content variant is not part of v1.

modules.description: The Fortran modules this file defines. Non-empty, and a module name is unique across the whole bundle (one .mod per module). The host resolves an entrypoint's / state_binding's `module` to the file (and thus member) that owns it via this list, and renders `use <module>, only: <symbol>` without parsing the source; tools/codegen_bundle.py:bundle_invariant_violations ties module attribution to file/member ownership.

compile_after.description: Optional. logical_paths of other bundle files that must compile before this one (e.g. this file `use`s a module another defines). It refines the role-precedence order that ROLE_BUILD_PRECEDENCE gives by default, so two files of the same role can be ordered. tools/codegen_bundle.py:bundle_invariant_violations checks that each entry resolves to another files[] entry, does not name the file itself, does not reverse ROLE_BUILD_PRECEDENCE (an edge may order within a role or agree with precedence, never a dependency of a later role), and forms no cycle; derive_build_graph topologically sorts by it. The host never infers these edges from the source — the bundle declares them.

entrypoints.description: The externally visible symbols of the unit. Non-empty. Together with state_bindings these are the structural anchors that replace signature-shape heuristics for identifying a node's published update path.

module.description: The Fortran module that publishes `symbol`, so the host renders `use <module>, only: <symbol>` mechanically. A file may define several modules or name them freely; the host never parses the source to discover which, so the bundle declares it. Fortran identifier grammar (<= 63 chars).

kind.description: operation = the member's published semantic operation (defined in a model-role file). checks_interface = the member's checks module surface (defined in a checks-role file).

defined_in.description: The logical_path of the files[] entry that defines this symbol. A helper / internal_module file can never appear here — that is the definition of 'private' in this contract.

target_lowering_plan.description: How the semantic IR is realized as execution algorithms for the target profile. A CLOSED key envelope with OPEN values: the key set is fixed here, and the interior of each object is left unconstrained in v1 (an A5 extension point a target backend fills in). state_residency other than 'host' requires the matching RESIDENCY capability in capability_requirements (x-required-capability-name), in addition to the one execution-model capability every bundle declares (checked by tools/codegen_bundle.py:bundle_invariant_violations).

fusion.description: Fusion groups over unit members. Each group's members must be a subset of optimization_unit.members.

capability_requirements.description: The harness capability ABI tokens this bundle requires. Duplicate-free, non-empty, and holding exactly one execution-model capability (sync_single_case / async_device_resident / batched_cases). A token whose name is outside CAPABILITY_VOCABULARY is a violation (fail-closed: an unrecognized capability is never assumed available). Matching against a harness manifest is by exact name@version; version ordering never implies compatibility.

state_bindings.description: How each member's primary state is reached. May be empty in v1. capture=checks_getter is the current M3c mechanism and takes capability: null; capture=harness_registration requires a state_registration@N token, which must also appear in capability_requirements (the Z6 shape — additive, no schema change). Agreement with the IR's algorithm.state_variables is checked at assembly time (Z2), where the IR is in scope.

module.description: The Fortran module that publishes storage_symbol, so the host renders `use <module>, only: <storage_symbol>` mechanically. A member may have several checks files with freely chosen module names; the host never parses the source to find the exporter. Fortran identifier grammar (<= 63 chars).

node_key.description: <spec_kind>/<spec_id>@<spec_version> (docs/GLOSSARY.md). The grammar matches the repository's canonical parser tools/orchestration_runtime.py:_parse_node_key_strict: a dot-separated lowercase spec_id and a [0-9][0-9A-Za-z._-]* version (a prerelease or short version, not only three numeric components).

capability_token.description: <capability_name>@<abi_version>. The name must be a member of CAPABILITY_VOCABULARY (tools/codegen_bundle.py); the version is an exact integer, never a range.

x-forbidden-examples.note: path escapes the assembled source root
the bundle carries no commands; the object is closed
the bundle carries no compiler flags; the target backend derives them
an entrypoint may not be defined in a private (helper / internal_module) file

x-forbidden-examples-note: This schema rejects on its own: the closed-key entries (an unknown top-level or per-object key), the unknown role, and — through the whole-path logical_path pattern — every traversal, separator, and normalization example ('../escape.f90', '/abs.f90', 'a/../b.f90', 'a//b.f90', 'dir\win.f90', './x.f90'). The canonical validator tools/codegen_bundle.py:validate_bundle rejects the rest, because no draft-07 pattern can carry them: the reserved build filenames ('Makefile'), the build/script extensions ('build.sh'), the per-language extension allowlist, the case-folded duplicate path ('A.f90' beside 'a.f90'), the closed capability vocabulary ('gpu_magic@1' matches the token pattern), and every cross-field invariant (entrypoint privacy and attribution, member coverage, the residency-capability coupling, object-name uniqueness). A consumer that applies this schema with a generic JSON Schema library obtains the field grammar only and must still call the canonical validator.
